MILITARY GOVERNMZNT O F SANTO DOMXNGO,
              Executive Order No. 669.
       AUTHORIZING VARIOTJS O F F I C E R S OF T H E
       DOMINICAN NATIONAL CONSTABULARY TO
        ADMINISTER OATHS I N CERTAIN CASES.
                   0. G. No. 3258.

     By virtue of the powers vested in the Military Government
of Santo Domingo, t h e following Evscutive Order is hereby pro-
mulgated :
     ARTICLE 1. The following commissioned officers of t h e
Dominican National Constabulary a r e hereby authorized t o ad-
minister oatbs where requir.c>d 01- authorized by law in matters
of official business pertainiiig to t h e Constabulary :
        Colonel Commandant, D. N. C.,
        Chief of Staff, D. N. C.,
        Intendant General, D. N. C.,
        Directors of Departments, D. N. C.,
        Inspectors of Districts, D. N. C.,
        Csmpany Commanders, D. N. C.,
     ARTICLE 2. No fee shall be charged f o r any oath admin-
istered under t h e provisions of this Law.
     ARTICLE 3. All1 laws or p a r t s of laws in conflict here-
with a r e hereby repealeld.
                                        S.S. ROBISON,
                          X e a r -A d mi r a 1, Unit ed S t a te s Navy,
                        Military Governor of Santo Domingo.

Santo Domingo, D. R.
September 17, 1921.
